item.text: I’ll follow the typed-spec workflow in this candidate only: first inspect the project shape and local instructions, then run the required game plan before touching source.

# CLAUDE.md

Use `AGENTS.md` as the authoritative local instructions.

In short: prefer `tn ... --json` for authoring, especially `tn scene`,
`tn ui`, `tn prefab set-material`, and `tn add` mechanic-block commands. Edit
durable source under `content/**` and `src/scripts/**` only when no command
covers the change, and do not repair generated bundle files.
Use `tn scene inspect arena --node <id> --project . --json` for one entity,
resource, system, prefab, or UI node before opening the full scene JSON.
For gameplay verification, run `tn iterate --project . --json` first; do not
run validate/build/playtest separately unless its compact diagnostic asks for
deeper proof.

{
  "name": "threenative-structured-source-starter",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "tn": "node bin/tn",
    "build": "tn authoring compile-typed-spec --json && tn build",
    "dev:web": "tn dev --target web",
    "iterate": "tn iterate --project . --json",
    "playtest": "tn playtest --scenario playtests/smoke-movement.playtest.json --stable-artifacts --json",
    "playtest:archetype": "tn playtest --scenario playtests/smoke-movement.playtest.json --stable-artifacts --json",
    "playtest:native": "tn playtest --scenario playtests/native-smoke-movement.playtest.json --stable-artifacts --json",
    "game:plan": "tn game plan --goal \"small arena collectible game\" --project . --apply --json",
    "game:improve": "tn game improve --apply-plan artifacts/game-production/plan.json --project . --json",
    "game:score": "tn game score --project . --json",
    "game:qa": "tn game qa --project . --run-proof --json",
    "game:release": "tn game release --project . --json",
    "recipe:controller": "tn recipe third-person-controller --scene arena --entity player --camera camera.main --dry-run --json",
    "validate": "tn authoring compile-typed-spec --json && tn validate",
    "validate:authoring": "tn authoring compile-typed-spec --json && tn authoring validate --json",
    "verify": "tn verify --frames 2 --json",
    "authoring:compile": "tn authoring compile-typed-spec --json"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@threenative/script-stdlib": "file:/home/joao/projects/threejs-to-bevy/packages/script-stdlib",
    "@threenative/sdk": "file:/home/joao/projects/threejs-to-bevy/packages/sdk"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@threenative/cli": "file:.threenative/cli",
    "@types/node": "^20.19.25",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3"
  }
}

item.text: I’m using the `threenative-workflow` skill because this is a generated ThreeNative game task with the typed-spec benchmark workflow layered on top.

item.aggregated_output:
---
name: threenative-workflow
description: Use the generated ThreeNative project workflow from Codex. Applies when editing structured source, adding gameplay, inspecting scenes, or verifying a generated ThreeNative project.
---

# ThreeNative Generated Project Workflow

Use `AGENTS.md` as the authoritative local instructions.

Default loop:

```bash
pnpm tn -- game plan --goal "<game idea>" --project . --json
pnpm tn -- cookbook list --json
pnpm tn -- project map --project . --json
pnpm tn -- iterate --project . --json
```

Prefer bounded source operations:

- `pnpm tn -- scene inspect <scene-id> --node <id> --project . --json`
- `pnpm tn -- add <mechanic-block> ... --project . --json`
- `pnpm tn -- material ... --project . --json`
- `pnpm tn -- ui ... --project . --json`

Do not use standalone validate/build/playtest as the normal verification loop.
If `tn iterate` reports a playtest issue, use:

```bash
pnpm tn -- playtest report --latest --scenario <name> --json
```

Keep durable edits in `content/**/*.json` and `src/scripts/**/*.ts`; do not edit
`dist/**`, emitted bundle JSON, or `scripts.bundle.js`.

item.text: Next I’m running the required non-mutating production plan and reading the typed spec so edits stay in the durable source contract first.

item.text: The starter spec is minimal: one player, one goal, and a score label. I’m checking the local typed-spec examples and generated content shape so the next edit can add the full collector loop in `src/game.spec.ts` without guessing field names.

item.text: There is already a portable player script and generated content, but the typed spec is the source of truth for this condition. I’m reading only the nearby generated files now to preserve the compiler’s expected shapes.

The generated content confirms compile output is very thin, so I’m checking the SDK type declarations before editing the typed spec. After that I’ll patch the spec and script, then compile the typed source.

item.text: I found the compiler maps typed `systems.script` entries into the structured system document, so the runtime collector loop can be declared from the spec. I’m applying a scoped patch now: five pickups, arena floor/walls, HUD resources/bindings, reset input, and one script export to drive movement, collection, win, and retry.

The compiler only writes scene/input/material documents, leaving the existing systems document intact. To keep both paths working, I’m making the old exported system delegate to the new collector update while the typed spec also declares that system in-scene.

The typed spec now owns the arena, five pickups, UI state, and script declaration. I’m updating the portable script next, keeping movement continuous and resource-driven so HUD bindings and retry state come from `GameState`.
I removed the previous one-axis starter script and am replacing it with the collector system export in the same file path the project already references.
The durable typed source and script are in place. I’m compiling `src/game.spec.ts` through the project-local `pnpm tn --` wrapper now, then I’ll run the generated iterate loop.
Typed-spec compilation passed and emitted the canonical scene, input, and material JSON. I’m starting the local verification loop now and will only dig deeper if its compact diagnostics ask for it.

item.text: `iterate` failed at the build step with a portable-script bundling rule: exported systems cannot reference module-local helpers/constants. I’m fixing that in the owning script by moving the constants and clamp logic inside the exported function, then I’ll rerun the same loop.
The script now keeps all emitted-system values inside the exported function body. I’m rerunning `pnpm tn -- iterate --project . --json` as requested by the diagnostic.

The next failure is structural: wall entities had `RigidBody` without `Collider`. I’m fixing the typed spec source by adding matching box colliders to the arena walls, then recompiling the spec before rerunning verification.
The collider fix is in the typed spec. I’m recompiling now so the generated scene JSON stays canonical before the next iterate run.
Recompile passed. I’m rerunning the same generated verification loop.

The compiler treats system `writes` as component names rather than entity IDs. I’m removing that typed-spec `writes` list so the script declaration no longer emits invalid component writes, then recompiling.

item.text: The analyzer also tracks `context.state(...)` as a resource write, and `CollectorState` was not declared in typed source. I’m folding those private fields into the existing `GameState` resource so the one declared resource owns score, win, retry, and collected flags.
I’m replacing the remaining private `CollectorState` usage with `GameState` resource fields, which matches the typed spec resource declaration.
The script now reads and writes only `GameState`, which is declared in `src/game.spec.ts`. I’m recompiling the typed spec and running iterate again.

item.text:
Implemented the typed-spec collector game in this candidate project.

Changed:
- `src/game.spec.ts`: durable typed authoring source for player, camera, arena floor/bounds, five pickups, `GameState` HUD fields, retry input, and collector system.
- `src/scripts/player.ts`: portable collector loop with WASD/arrow movement, pickup collection, score/progress updates, win state, and R/Enter retry.

Verification passed:
- `pnpm tn -- authoring compile-typed-spec --project . --json`
- `pnpm tn -- iterate --project . --json` returned `TN_ITERATE_OK`

Artifacts:
- `artifacts/iterate/latest/report.json`
- `artifacts/iterate/latest/screenshot.png`
- `artifacts/iterate/latest/playtest/contact-sheet.png`
